comment: BB2001 measurement records (companion to scripts/measure/*.js, mirroring the goldlogs.json pattern). Each record names the src/config.ts constant it informs, so the audit trail runs source -> record -> src/data/bb2001.ts -> the constant. Geometry records are in SOURCE pixels (BB2001 is 640x480). Measured off the public YouTube capture YMmqNpmA60U by frame-stepping in-browser with full canvas pixel access (the video was untainted), then fitting features with the scripts/measure/lib.js math (affinity, RANSAC line fits by hand in the browser). Method notes and the perspective finding are written up in docs/research/backyard-2001-video-notes.md.

source.note: Public gameplay capture, 640x480. Canvas pixel access confirmed untainted, so geometry was measured objectively (dirt/grass boundary scans + RANSAC foul-line fits), not eyeballed.

affinityTest.what: Is BB2001's field an affine squash of the diamond (like ours) or true perspective? A diamond is a square, so its 4 bases are a square's corners; affine maps preserve diagonal bisection, perspective does not.
affinityTest.method: Wide reset frame t=430. Foul lines fit by RANSAC (white chalk, occlusion-robust): left slope 1.239, right 1.238, symmetric, intersecting at home (319,444). Bases located by dirt/grass boundary scans: 2B apex down the symmetry axis, 1B/3B as the outer infield-dirt corners along each foul line (symmetric: y=286/287, x=513.6/126.2). Robust to camera pan/zoom, which is affine and cannot convert perspective<->affine.

clips.note: quiet post-play wide view, everyone reset

affinityTest.informs: src/art/projection.ts (which maps y 1:1 -- NO vertical foreshortening). BB matching would need a render-only y'=f(y). Gated behind the graphics phase; does NOT affect pace.
affinityTest.nNote: n=1 frame, but the measurement is objective (pixel boundaries, not eyeball) and the gap is 11x the threshold, so a second frame would confirm rather than decide. Left/right symmetry (1.239 vs 1.238; bases mirror about x=319.9) is the internal consistency check.

foulSlope.what: Near-field lateral foreshortening: horizontal run per unit depth along the foul lines from home.
foulSlope.method: RANSAC fit of white chalk foul-line pixels, refit on inliers.
foulSlope.informs: src/systems/geometry.ts FOUL_SLOPE. Our near-field lateral squash already matches BB within ~3%; the difference from BB is the DEPTH compression (perspective), not the foul angle.

pace.note: Pending. The pace shot list (home->1B x6, fly hang x6, fielder/throw/CPU delays, pitch corridor) is the next measurement pass and the one that fixes the 'too fast' complaint. No pace records yet -- deliberately not seeding from the existing n=1 notes.
